title: | Raleigh Hills Business Association
description: For membership requests/application please send us an email request to info@RaleighHillsBusinessAssn.org and we will email you an application OR at the bottom of this home page there is a link to our "RHBA Application For Membership". Fill out the membership application. New membership is $100. New memberships are prorated throughout the year with renewals due
htmlMain:
For membership requests/application please send us an email request to info@RaleighHillsBusinessAssn.org and we will email you an application OR at the bottom of this home page there is a link to our "RHBA Application For Membership".

Fill out the membership application. New membership is $100. New memberships are prorated throughout the year with renewals due on June 1st of each year.

MEMBER SPOTLIGHTS VIA ZOOM - EVERY 2nd WEDNESDAY 8-9:00 AM

May: Jon Cohen, Jon Cohen@Windermere Realty Trust. Focusing on Jon's experience as a lawyer, helping navigate 1st time home buyers. Jon will also be sharing his experience. passion and involvement with Positive Charge!PDX

Thank you Ruthann Marquis our April member presentation, Valley Community Presbyterian Earth Care Team Leader.

6b39e4bae1b545998293c425bccbeb6e

SAVE THE DATE

Our 14th Annual Recycle/Shred Event is April 18, 2026

Print a Flyer

WE HAVE IT COVERED!! A BIG THANK YOU for our Sponsors to generously donated toward the Shred Truck rental of $750.

The event hours: 9 am to Noon in the AAA Parking lot on 8555 SW Apple Way, PDX 97225

RHBA Sponsored Shred truck from Shred NW - QR Code Donation Link

Secure shred for a maximum of  THREE bags or banker boxes PER vehicle.

More than 3 boxes? Please be mindful and an extra donation will help to cover the cost of  your extra-shred items.

To support this event cash donations will be accepted.

Those Member business who will taking recycle/shred this year are:

    • SBC Recycle - accepting eWaste at N/C, minimal charge for light bulbs/florescent tubes and batteries   Electronic Recycling Data Destruction SBC Recycle
    • Community Warehouse will be accepting usable home-goods    Home - Community Warehouse
    • Garden Home/West Slope Community Libraries will be taking bottles and cans      Garden Home and West Slope Community Libraries
    • Neighborhood House with Two Can Tuesday will be accepting canned and non-perishable food, pet food, AND  hygiene products     Home - Neighborhood House
    • Jon Cohen Realty with Positive Charge! PDX's Period Power Project - menstrual products. Benefiting local non-profits for more specifics about the Period Power Project visit http://www.positivechargepdx.org/periodpower2026
    • James Recycling will be accepting items at New Seasons Milwaukie Store     Home - James Recycling

  Tandem Recycling Event: Same Day/Same Time

    • Valley Community Presbyterian will be have their own recycle event: it's an opportunity to park your car and walk through and talk to the different earth friendly business in the greater Portland area. Drop off books/media and games, kid’ clothes for the BSD, textiles, athletic shoes, pet supplies, luggage/duffels and backpacks for foster kids, toiletries/socks/blankets for the vets, medical equipment, plastic film, cans and bottles, and non-perishable food. Paper shredding will be in the AAA parking lot as well as furniture/household goods and electronic recycling.  Events — Valley Community Presbyterian

Questions or concerns or to volunteer contact: Bev Snow-Kuehn 503.452.0071 or send a message to info@RaleighHillsBusinessAssn.org

RHBA ZOOM MEETINGS ARE OPEN TO THE PUBLIC

Our member/speaker meeting is the 2nd Wednesday of each Month at 8:00-9:00am

At these meetings we showcase a RHBA member. Learn about what's happening in our businesses and community.

Visitors are welcome

8am to 9am Please Join us On
Zoom

RHBA Board Zoom meeting the 4th Wednesday of the month from 8:00 - 9:00am, unless there is a special request for a rare exception.

BUSINESS BUILDER - Let's help our members grow their businesses.

EXCITING OPPORTUNITY! WE WILL BE HAVING OUR 2nd COFFEE MEET-UP IN APRIL AT LA PROVENCE

APRIL HAS 5 WEDNESDAYS

WEDNESDAY, APRIL 29th from 10-11:30 on the patio.

Place your order at the bakery bar. Find our RHBA Group and La Provence Staff will deliver your order to your table.

RHBA COFFEE MEETUP - The 1st Wednesday of the month.

  • 10:00-11:00am
  • At the Coffee Cart inside Westside Wines next to Advance Camera - 8122 SW BHH/97225

~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~

Every 3rd Wednesday

No-Host Lunch Meetup Noon to 1pm

Eastern Pearl, 8651 SW Canyon Drive, Portland OR 97225

Sign up for our email and get notified for all events 

RHBA Outreach/Events

  • SAVE THE DATE

  • 14th Annual RECYCLE/SHRED EVENT

  • SATURDAY, APRIL 18, 2026 - 9am to Noon

Donations can be paid by using our QR code, check or cash. Let Mary or Bev know if you plan to donate or email us at info@RaleighHillsBusinessAssn.org

~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
RHBA (pronounced Ree-ba) is active in our community in projects where you can help with donations to the projects such as the  School Supply Drive, the Shop With A Cop with Washington County Sheriff's Office and Beaverton School District, and our annual Shred and Recycling Event!
 

MEMBER BULLETIN BOARD: On-going or Up-coming Events

  • Westside Wines - Tastings Every Saturday from 11:00am-4:00pm - Visit Westside Wines for new wine tasting schedule/calendar and their New Wine Selections.  Sign up for their newsletter and keep in the loop, they always offer monthly specials, tastings, etc. https://www.westsidewinespdx.com/
  • West Slope Library  - Every Tuesday at 10:00am, Family Story Time for kids and teens.
  • Valley Community Presbyterian Church Earth Care Team Non-Perishable Food Collection and Styrofoam On-going Recycling every 1st and 3rd Thursday, 10:00 - 11:30am. East Parking lot. Food donations benefit St. Matthew's Food Pantry. Valley Earth Care Team requests a monetary donation when dropping off Styrofoam.  https://www.valleycommunity.org/events
  • Salute Our Veterans On-going Signature Program "Operation Vet Pack" reach out to Agnes 971-910-0670 for more info, how you can help or what you can donate to this program helping serve our Homeless Veterans.
  • Fine Arts Starts - Visit their website to learn about their ongoing offerings; ART Camp, Classes & Workshops, Studio, et al.  Sign up for Art Camp 2026 https://www.fineartstarts.com/art-camp.html 
  • Elegantly Frugal Creations by Toni Curry gallery showing. Gigi's Cafe in Hillsdale and Jan's Bookstore. Do you have some wall-space in your office that could use brightening? Contact Toni to see what art pieces are available to hang in your office.  Toni's cell: 503-730-8869.
  • Portland Beaverton Title Boxing Club - Visit site for classes and Memberships
  • Jan's and Arcade J&A Bookstore is selling our "Gateway to the Past" RHBA's celebrating the mural, a colorful journey. We are excited to announce there are copies in stock. Please visit the store in Scholls Crossing to pick up a few, great for gifting.
  • March - April - Mudville Clay Studio - Book Now Classes for all ages, 6 years to adult.  Some scholarships available w/lottery system application here: https://forms.gle/8ZcG2iMmiZYJJ8Ss6
  • April 26th - 11:00 AM - 4:00 PM Portland Clay House Pottery Sale. Members will be selling their hand made pottery. 4770 SW 76th Avenue/PDX 97225
  • April 26th - St. John's Bee the Light Fundraising Event. Proceeds will benefit the church and 2 ministry partners, Western Farm Workers Assn and William Temple House for mental health counseling, food and other social services. For those who want to support the fundraiser with items for the auction or monetary donations. Business who make donations will be recognized in the auction program. https://parishofstjohn.ejoinme.org/BEE
  • ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
  • Mailing Address: 10445 SW Canyon Rd, Suite 116, Beaverton OR 97005
  • Membership Benefits
  • RHBA Application For Membership
  • RHBA Renewal
  • Email: info@raleighhillsbusinessassn.org
Our MISSION is to support the community and neighborhoods surrounding Raleigh Hills by promoting a vibrant business community, maintaining a safe and healthy environment, create community awareness and maximize livability.
RHBA is not a leads group.  We do not share your information with other businesses/organizations. We are all about community building!
